**Example 2.9.13.** The algebra $U(\mathcal{H})$, where $\mathcal{H}$ is the Heisenberg Lie algebra, is the algebra generated by $x$, $y$, $c$ with the relations

$$yx - xy = c, \qquad yc - cy = 0, \qquad xc - cx = 0.$$

Note that the Weyl algebra is the quotient of $U(\mathcal{H})$ by the relation $c = 1$.
